description:
The libreswan package provides an implementation of IPsec and IKE, which permits the creation of secure tunnels over untrusted networks. The libreswan package can be installed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf install libreswan
rationale:
Providing the ability for remote users or systems
to initiate a secure VPN connection protects information when it is
transmitted over a wide area network.
ocil_clause: the libreswan package is not installed
ocil:
Run the following command to determine if the libreswan package is installed: 
$ rpm -q libreswan
fixtext:
To install the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 package libreswan run the following command:

$ sudo dnf install libreswan
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must have the libreswan package installed.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 libreswan package must be installed.
stig.vuldiscussion: Providing the ability for remote users or systems to initiate a secure VPN connection protects information when it is transmitted over a wide area network.
stig.checktext:
Verify that Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 libreswan service package is installed.

Check that the libreswan service package is installed with the following command:

$ dnf list --installed libreswan

Example output:

libreswan.x86_64          4.6-3.el9

If the "libreswan" package is not installed, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Install the libreswan service (if it is not already installed) with the following command:

$ sudo dnf install libreswan

title: Install libreswan Package
